slides.title: Food and Health
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 4 English, Unit 3: Food and Health. Food is one of Allah's great blessings. In this unit you will learn how to talk about food and health in English. You will read about where food comes from, how to keep it clean and safe, and why fresh food helps your body and your brain. You will learn to give clear instructions, ask good questions, and compare healthy and unhealthy choices. Along the way you will meet many new words — from sandwich and rice to pesticide and famine — and you will use grammar that helps you explain rules, reasons, and comparisons. By the end, you will be ready to read this unit's story, “The Bitter Lunch”, about a boy named Musa who learns to check his food with care. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Name and describe many foods, and talk about where food comes from
Explain why fresh, clean food is good for your body and your brain
Give clear instructions using imperatives (“Wash the fruit.”)
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to name and describe many foods, and talk about where food comes from. you will also explain why fresh, clean food is good for your body and your brain. you will also give clear instructions using imperatives (“wash the fruit.”). Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: sandwich, lamb, rice, spice, bakery, fresh. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes From Farm to Plate and A Poster on the Wall. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Modal Verbs — must and might
bullets: Modal verbs are special helping verbs. In this unit we use two of them: must and might. Must shows that something is necessary or very important — a rule....
slides.narration: The first language focus is Modal Verbs — must and might. Modal verbs are special helping verbs. In this unit we use two of them: must and might. Must shows that something is necessary or very important — a rule. Might shows that something is possible, but not certain. Knowing both helps you explain rules and possibilities clearly. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Writing 1: How to Make a Sandwich (Instructions)
Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: My Favourite Meal. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
